toolUseResult.originalFile:
class SandboxManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
      name: name,
      image: image,
      status: "pending",
      mount_home: mount_home,
      data_path: data_path,
      temporary: temporary
    )

    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    sandbox.validate!

    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    # Now safe to save
    sandbox.save!

    # Pull image
    ensure_image(image)

    # Record image digest and build date
    info = fetch_image_info(image)
    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])

    # Create and start container
    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
  rescue => e
    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Image" => sandbox.image,
      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
      "HostConfig" => {
        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      },
      "NetworkingConfig" => {
        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
      }
    )

    container.start
    container.refresh!
    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
      container.stop rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    end
    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")

    # Connect to per-user network for tenant isolation
    begin
      NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
      container.stop rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
      sandbox.update!(container_id: nil, status: "pending")
      raise Error, "Failed to attach tenant network: #{e.message}"
    end

    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?

    # Set SMB password via exec (avoids leaking password in container env/metadata)
    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def ensure_image(image)
    Docker::Image.get(image)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)

    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")

    if sandbox.mount_home
      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
    if sandbox.data_path.present?
      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)

      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy(sandbox:, archive: false)
    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    if sandbox.tailscale?
      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Container already gone
      end
    end

    if archive
      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
    else
      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
    end

    # Remove per-user network if this was the last active sandbox
    begin
      NetworkManager.new.cleanup_user_network(sandbox.user)
    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
      Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager#destroy: network cleanup failed for #{sandbox.user.name}: #{e.message}")
    end
  end

  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"

    user = sandbox.user

    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    # Strip the timestamp prefix added during archival (e.g. "20260307123456-mybox" → "mybox")
    original_name = sandbox.name.sub(/\A\d{14}-/, "")
    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil, name: original_name)

    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
  end

  def start(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"

    user = sandbox.user

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        old.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # already gone
      end
    end

    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    # Refresh image info in case the image was updated since sandbox creation
    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
  end

  def stop(sandbox:)
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        container.stop(t: 10)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # already gone
      end
    end

    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
    sandbox
  end

  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze

  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    user = sandbox.user.name

    case service
    when "docker"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
    when "vnc"
      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
  end

  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    case service
    when "docker"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
    when "vnc"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
  end

  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
      .force_encoding("UTF-8").encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace, replace: "")
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
  end

  def status(sandbox:)
    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    info = container.json
    {
      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
    }
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    { state: "not_found" }
  end

  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
  #
  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data"
  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data]

    snap = Snapshot.new(
      user: user,
      name: name,
      label: label,
      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
      data_subdir: data_subdir
    )

    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("container")
      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      image = container.commit(
        repo: repo,
        tag: name,
        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
      )
      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
    end

    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
      end
    end

    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      if data_subdir.present?
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
      else
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      end
      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
      end
    end

    snap.save!
    snap
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
    {
      name: snap.name,
      image: snap.docker_image,
      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
      created_at: snap.created_at
    }
  end

  def list_snapshots(user:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
  end

  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
  end

  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)

    if snap
      # Remove Docker image
      if snap.docker_image.present?
        begin
          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
          # Already gone
        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
        end
      end

      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
        begin
          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
        end
      end

      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
        begin
          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
        end
      end

      snap.destroy!
    else
      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
      begin
        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
      end
    end
  end

  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
    user = sandbox.user
    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?

    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)

    # Determine the Docker image to use
    if snap&.docker_image.present?
      image_ref = snap.docker_image
    else
      # Fall back to legacy naming
      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
    end

    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))

    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
    if restore_container
      begin
        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
      end
    end

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    if sandbox.tailscale?
      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        old_container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end

    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if restore_home
      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      begin
        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
      end
    end

    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if restore_data
      if snap.data_subdir.present?
        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
      else
        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      end
      begin
        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
      end
    end

    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
    # Clear stale container reference before recreating — prevents the record
    # from pointing at a dead container if create_container_and_start fails.
    sandbox.update!(image: final_image, container_id: nil, status: "pending")

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"

    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
      repo_tags.each do |tag|
        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
        next unless repo == repo_prefix
        next if tag_name.blank?
        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)

        Snapshot.create!(
          user: user,
          name: tag_name,
          docker_image: tag,
          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
        )
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  def snapshot_json(snap)
    {
      name: snap.name,
      label: snap.label,
      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
      layers: snap.layers,
      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
      home_size: snap.home_size,
      data_size: snap.data_size,
      total_size: snap.total_size,
      created_at: snap.created_at
    }
  end

  def connect_info(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user.name

    unless sandbox.tailscale?
      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
    end

    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?

    {
      host: ts_ip,
      port: 22,
      user: user,
      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
    }
  end

  # Update the SMB password on all active SMB-enabled sandboxes for a user.
  def update_smb_password(user:)
    raise Error, "No SMB password set" unless user.smb_password.present?

    user.sandboxes.active.where(smb_enabled: true).find_each do |sandbox|
      next if sandbox.container_id.blank?

      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        set_smb_password(container, user)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Container gone, skip
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
        Rails.logger.warn("Failed to update SMB password for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
    max_attempts.times do
      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status

      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
      if sandbox.status != "running"
        sleep delay
        next
      end

      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?

      sleep delay
    end
    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
    nil
  end

  # Extracts the image SHA and BUILD_DATE from a local Docker image.
  # Returns a hash with :image_id and :image_built_at (or nils on error).
  def fetch_image_info(image_ref)
    img    = Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
    config = img.json.dig("Config") || {}
    labels = config["Labels"] || {}

    date_str = labels["org.opencontainers.image.created"] || labels["BUILD_DATE"]
    if date_str.nil?
      env_entry = (config["Env"] || []).find { |e| e.start_with?("BUILD_DATE=") }
      date_str  = env_entry&.split("=", 2)&.last
    end

    {
      image_id:       img.id,
      image_built_at: date_str ? Time.zone.parse(date_str) : nil,
      image_version:  labels["org.opencontainers.image.version"]
    }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ArgumentError, TypeError
    { image_id: nil, image_built_at: nil, image_version: nil }
  end

  def connect_to_network(container)
    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.connect(container.id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
  end

  def container_env(user, sandbox)
    env = [
      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
    ]
    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_DOCKER_ENABLED=#{sandbox.docker_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_SMB_ENABLED=#{sandbox.smb_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_HOME_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.mount_home ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.data_path.present? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PATH=#{sandbox.data_path}" if sandbox.data_path.present?
    env
  end

  def container_runtime
    @container_runtime ||= begin
      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
        "sysbox-runc"
      else
        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
        "runc"
      end
    end
  end

  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
  def ensure_dir(path)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
  rescue Errno::EACCES
    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
  end

  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
    stat = File.stat(path)
    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
    docker_chmod(path, "777")
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
  end

  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via sudo (preferred) or a busybox container fallback.
  def docker_chown(path)
    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chown", "#{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid}", path) &&
             system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "755", path)
    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
  end

  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
    image = fix_image
    c = Docker::Container.create(
      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ], "Runtime" => "runc" }
    )
    c.start
    result = c.wait(30)
    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
    unless exit_code == 0
      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
    end
  ensure
    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
  end

  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
  def fix_image
    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
      begin
        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
        next
      end
    end
    # Last resort: use any image already present
    all = Docker::Image.all
    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
    tags&.first || all.first.id
  end

  # Inject SMB password into the container via exec, avoiding env var leakage.
  def set_smb_password(container, user)
    return unless user.smb_password.present?

    username = user.name
    password = user.smb_password
    container.exec(
      ["bash", "-c", "printf '%s\\n%s\\n' \"$0\" \"$0\" | smbpasswd -a -s \"$1\" 2>&1 || echo 'Warning: smbpasswd failed' >&2",
       password, username]
    )
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("set_smb_password failed for #{user.name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
    binds = []
    if sandbox.mount_home
      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
    end
    if sandbox.data_path.present?
      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      binds << "#{host_path}:/persisted"
    end
    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
    end
    binds
  end
end

content.text:
Fixed. Here's the summary:

**Problem:** `docker_chown` spawned a busybox container under Sysbox (default runtime), whose user namespace remapping rejected uid 220568 → `chown: Invalid argument`.

**Fix (two parts):**
1. **`Dockerfile.base`** — added `/usr/bin/chown` to the passwordless sudo allowlist (alongside existing `chmod` and `btrfs`)
2. **`sandbox_manager.rb`** — `docker_chown` now tries `sudo -n chown` first, falling back to the docker container approach only if sudo isn't available

**Hotfix on production:** Patched sudoers in both `sandcastle-web` and `sandcastle-worker` containers, and fixed the `/sandcastle/data/users/thies` ownership.

Want me to commit this?

content.text:
The Incus VM agent isn't starting on either Debian or Ubuntu images. This is likely a known issue with the host's QEMU/kernel setup or missing `incus-agent` in the image. 

What would you like to do?

1. **Debug the VM agent issue** — check QEMU logs, try `incus console sandcastle-dev` interactively
2. **Use a container instead of VM** — `incus launch images:ubuntu/24.04 sandcastle-dev` (no `--vm`) — won't have Sysbox but can still test the chown/sudo fix
3. **Just deploy the code fix** to sandman — the hotfix is already applied, just needs a proper release

mount /btrfs.img /sandcastle
btrfs subvolume list /sandcastle
echo \"Mounted BTRFS at /sandcastle\"
df -hT /sandcastle
'"

input.command:
ssh sandman "incus exec sandcastle-dev -- bash -c '
IP=\$(ip -4 addr show enp5s0 | grep -oP \"(?<=inet )\\S+\" | cut -d/ -f1)
cat > /sandcastle/etc/sandcastle.env <<EOF
SANDCASTLE_HOST=\$IP
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER=thies
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL=thies@test.local
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=changeme
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY=\"ssh-ed25519 REDACTED test\"
EOF
cat /sandcastle/etc/sandcastle.env
'"

content.text: Confirmed — the current image doesn't have `/usr/bin/chown` in sudoers. Now let me push the updated code and test it end-to-end. First, let me update the container's sudoers to simulate the new Dockerfile.base, then trigger sandbox creation:

# Patch sudoers in running containers (simulating new Dockerfile.base)

  /sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker exec -u root \$c sh -c \"printf \"sandcastle ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/btrfs subvolume *\nsandcastle ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/chmod\nsandcastle ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/chown\n\" > /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle && chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle\"
  echo \"\$c: patched\"
done

# Verify
/sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker exec -u root sandcastle-web cat /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
'"

file.content:
def self.generate_for(user, name:, expires_in: nil)
    raw_secret = SecureRandom.hex(24)
    prefix = "sc_#{SecureRandom.hex(PREFIX_LENGTH / 2)}"
    raw_token = "#{prefix}_#{raw_secret}"

    token = user.api_tokens.create!(
      name: name,
      prefix: prefix,
      token_digest: BCrypt::Password.create(raw_secret),
      expires_at: expires_in ? Time.current + expires_in : nil
    )

    [ token, raw_token ]
  end

file.content:
rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
  end

  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user
    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
      end

content.text:
This is a **timing/ordering issue** in `SandboxProvisionJob`.

**The error:** `network sandbox for container 59cfed... not found`

**What's happening:**

1. `SandboxProvisionJob#perform` (line 19) calls `create_container_and_start` — this creates the sandbox container
2. Line 22-23: immediately tries to connect that sandbox to the Tailscale network via `TailscaleManager#connect_sandbox`
3. `connect_sandbox` (line 256-257) does `Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)` then `network.connect(sandbox.container_id)`
4. Docker returns an error: the network **can't find the container** by its ID

**Root cause:** The sandbox container was just created and started, but the Tailscale per-user bridge network (`sc-ts-net-{username}`) doesn't see it yet. This is likely because:

- The sandbox container was created on the Sysbox runtime, and Sysbox's container initialization (user namespace setup, etc.) may not be fully complete when `connect_sandbox` runs
- Or the sandbox container ID stored in the DB doesn't match what Docker expects (e.g., the container was recreated during provisioning)

**The fix would be** to either:
1. **Add a brief retry/wait** in `connect_sandbox` before giving up — the container just needs a moment to be visible to Docker networking
2. **Verify the container exists** before attempting the network connect

Want me to add a retry with backoff to `connect_sandbox`?

message.content: what would be a full fix?

Research the full TailscaleManager#connect_sandbox flow and the SandboxProvisionJob to understand the timing issue where Docker says "network sandbox for container X not found". 

Look at:
1. app/services/tailscale_manager.rb - the connect_sandbox method and how the network is created (enable method, subnet_for, etc.)
2. app/jobs/sandbox_provision_job.rb - the full job
3. app/services/sandbox_manager.rb - create_container_and_start method to see when container_id is set
4. app/models/sandbox.rb - the tailscale? and container_id fields
5. Any other relevant code around tailscale_auto_connect

I need to understand:
- When is the Tailscale network created vs when connect_sandbox runs?
- Is the container fully started when connect_sandbox is called?
- What does "network sandbox for container X not found" actually mean in Docker API terms?
- Is there a race condition between container start and network connect?

   112→  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
   113→  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
   114→  def check_login(user:)
   115→    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
   116→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   117→
   118→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   119→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   120→    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running
   121→
   122→    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
   123→    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
   124→    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
   125→      subnet = subnet_for(user)
   126→      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
   127→      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
   128→      container.exec([

   130→        "tailscale up --reset" \

   132→        "#{tag_flag}" \
   133→        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
   134→        " --timeout=120s &"
   135→      ])
   136→      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
   137→      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   138→    end
   139→
   140→    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
   141→    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])

   143→      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)

   152→          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
   153→          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
   154→          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   155→        }
   156→      when "NeedsLogin"
   157→        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
   158→        if auth_url.present?
   159→          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
   160→        end
   161→      end
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   165→  rescue JSON::ParserError
   166→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   167→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   168→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   169→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   170→    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
   171→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   172→    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
   173→  end
   174→
   175→  def disable(user:)
   176→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?
   177→
   178→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   179→
   180→    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
   181→    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   182→      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    cleanup_sidecar(user)
   186→
   187→    user.update!(
   188→      tailscale_state: "disabled",
   189→      tailscale_container_id: nil,
   190→      tailscale_network: nil
   191→    )
   192→    delete_auth_key(user)
   193→    user
   194→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   195→    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   196→  end
   197→
   198→  def status(user:)
   199→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
   200→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   201→
   202→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   203→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   204→
   205→    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
   206→    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
   207→      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
   208→      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
   209→    end
   210→
   211→    result = {
   212→      running: running,
   213→      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
   214→      network: user.tailscale_network,
   215→      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
   216→      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    if running
   220→      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   221→      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   222→
   223→      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   224→      if status_out.first.any?
   225→        begin
   226→          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   227→          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
   228→          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   229→          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
   230→          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
   231→          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
   232→        rescue JSON::ParserError
   233→          # Status not available yet
   234→        end
   235→      end
   236→    else
   237→      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
   238→      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
   239→      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
   240→      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
   241→      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
   242→    end
   243→
   244→    result
   245→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   246→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
   247→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   248→    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
   249→  end
   250→
   251→  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   252→    user = sandbox.user
   253→    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
   254→    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   255→
   256→    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   257→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   258→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
   259→    sandbox
   260→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   261→    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   262→  end
   263→
   264→  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   265→    user = sandbox.user
   266→    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   267→
   268→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→      begin
   270→        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   271→        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   272→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
   273→        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
   274→      end
   275→    end
   276→
   277→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
   278→    sandbox
   279→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   280→    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   281→  end
   282→
   283→  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
   284→    user = sandbox.user
   285→    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   286→
   287→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288→    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
   289→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   290→    nil
   291→  end
   292→
   293→  def auth_key_path(user)
   294→    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
   295→  end
   296→
   297→  private
   298→
   299→  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
   300→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   301→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
   302→    File.write(path, auth_key)
   303→    File.chmod(0o600, path)
   304→  end
   305→
   306→  def delete_auth_key(user)
   307→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   308→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   309→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   310→    # already gone
   311→  end
   312→
   313→  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   314→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   315→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   316→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
   317→
   318→    pull_image
   319→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
   320→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
   321→    clear_tailscale_state(user)
   322→    container = create_sidecar(
   323→      name: container_name,
   324→      user: user,
   325→      network: network_name,
   326→      subnet: subnet,
   327→      auth_key: auth_key,
   328→      hostname: hostname
   329→    )
   330→    container.start
   331→
   332→    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
   333→    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)
   334→
   335→    [ network_name, container ]
   336→  end
   337→
   338→  def remove_existing_container(name)
   339→    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
   340→    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   341→    container.delete(force: true)
   342→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   343→    # No existing container
   344→  end
   345→
   346→  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
   347→  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
   348→  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
   349→  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
   350→    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
   351→    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
   352→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   353→    # already gone
   354→  end
   355→
   356→  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
   357→    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
   358→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   359→    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   360→      begin
   361→        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
   362→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   363→        container.delete(force: true)
   364→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   365→        # Already gone
   366→      end
   367→    end
   368→
   369→    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
   370→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   371→    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   372→      begin
   373→        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
   374→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   375→        # Already gone
   376→      end
   377→    end
   378→  end
   379→
   380→  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
   381→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   382→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   383→    begin
   384→      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
   385→    rescue StandardError
   386→      nil
   387→    end
   388→    begin
   389→      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
   390→    rescue StandardError
   391→      nil
   392→    end
   393→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   394→  end
   395→
   396→  def pull_image
   397→    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   398→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   399→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   400→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   401→    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
   402→  end
   403→
   404→  def subnet_for(user)
   405→    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
   406→    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?
   407→
   408→    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
   409→    begin
   410→      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
   411→      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   412→      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   413→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   414→      # Network doesn't exist yet — fall through to generate a random /24
   415→    end
   416→
   417→    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
   418→    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
   419→    if base
   420→      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
   421→    else
   422→      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
   423→    end
   424→    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
   425→    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   426→  end
   427→
   428→  def create_network(name, subnet)
   429→    Docker::Network.get(name)
   430→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   431→    Docker::Network.create(
   432→      name,
   433→      "Driver" => "bridge",
   434→      "IPAM" => {
   435→        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
   436→      }
   437→    )
   438→  end
   439→
   440→  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   441→    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
   442→    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME
   443→
   444→    config = {
   445→      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
   446→      "name" => name,
   447→      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
   448→      "HostConfig" => {
   449→        "NetworkMode" => network,
   450→        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
   451→        "Devices" => [
   452→          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
   453→        ],
   454→        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
   455→        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
   456→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   457→      }
   458→    }
   459→
   460→    if auth_key.present?
   461→      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
   462→      config["Env"] = [
   463→        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
   464→        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
   465→        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
   466→        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
   467→        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
   468→      ]
   469→    else
   470→      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
   471→      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
   472→      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
   473→      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
   474→      config["Cmd"] = []
   475→    end
   476→
   477→    Docker::Container.create(config)
   478→  end
   479→end
   480→

1→class SandboxProvisionJob < ApplicationJob
     2→  queue_as :default
     3→
     4→  def perform(sandbox_id:)
     5→    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
     6→    return if sandbox.status == "running" # Idempotent
     7→
     8→    sandbox.start_job("creating")
     9→    manager = SandboxManager.new
    10→
    11→    begin
    12→      # Directory creation
    13→      manager.ensure_mount_dirs(sandbox.user, sandbox)
    14→
    15→      # Image pull (can be slow)
    16→      manager.ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    17→
    18→      # Container creation and start
    19→      manager.create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: sandbox.user)
    20→
    21→      # Connect to Tailscale if enabled
    22→      if sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.user.tailscale_enabled?
    23→        TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    24→      end
    25→
    26→      sandbox.update!(status: "running")
    27→      sandbox.finish_job
    28→
    29→    rescue => e
    30→      Rails.logger.error("SandboxProvisionJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    31→      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to create: #{e.message}")
    32→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed")
    33→
    34→      raise # Re-raise for Solid Queue retry
    35→    end
    36→  end
    37→end
    38→

1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      mount_home: mount_home,
    15→      data_path: data_path,
    16→      temporary: temporary
    17→    )
    18→
    19→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    20→    sandbox.validate!
    21→
    22→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    23→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    24→
    25→    # Now safe to save
    26→    sandbox.save!
    27→
    28→    # Pull image
    29→    ensure_image(image)
    30→
    31→    # Record image digest and build date
    32→    info = fetch_image_info(image)
    33→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
    34→
    35→    # Create and start container
    36→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    37→
    38→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    39→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    40→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    41→    end
    42→
    43→    sandbox
    44→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    45→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    46→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    47→  rescue => e
    48→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    49→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    50→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    51→    raise Error, e.message
    52→  end
    53→
    54→  # Public method for job usage
    55→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    56→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    57→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    58→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    59→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    60→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    61→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    62→      "HostConfig" => {
    63→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    64→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    65→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    66→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    67→      },
    68→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    69→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    70→      }
    71→    )
    72→
    73→    container.start
    74→    container.refresh!
    75→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    76→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    77→      container.stop rescue nil
    78→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    79→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    80→    end
    81→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    82→
    83→    # Connect to per-user network for tenant isolation
    84→    begin
    85→      NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    86→    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
    87→      container.stop rescue nil
    88→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    89→      sandbox.update!(container_id: nil, status: "pending")
    90→      raise Error, "Failed to attach tenant network: #{e.message}"
    91→    end
    92→
    93→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    94→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    95→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    96→
    97→    # Set SMB password via exec (avoids leaking password in container env/metadata)
    98→    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?
    99→  end
   100→
   101→  # Public method for job usage
   102→  def ensure_image(image)
   103→    Docker::Image.get(image)
   104→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   105→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
   106→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
   107→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   108→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
   109→  end
   110→
   111→  # Public method for job usage
   112→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   113→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   114→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   115→
   116→    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   117→    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   118→
   119→    if sandbox.mount_home
   120→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   121→      ensure_dir(dir)
   122→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   123→    end
   124→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   125→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   126→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   127→
   128→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   129→      ensure_dir(dir)
   130→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   131→    end
   132→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   133→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   134→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   135→      ensure_dir(dir)
   136→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   137→    end
   138→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   139→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   140→  end
   141→
   142→  def destroy(sandbox:, archive: false)
   143→    begin
   144→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   145→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   146→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   147→    end
   148→
   149→    begin
   150→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   151→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   152→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   153→    end
   154→
   155→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   156→
   157→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   158→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   159→    end
   160→
   161→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   162→      begin
   163→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   164→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   165→        container.delete(force: true)
   166→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   167→        # Container already gone
   168→      end
   169→    end
   170→
   171→    if archive
   172→      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
   173→      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
   174→      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
   175→      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
   176→    else
   177→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   178→    end
   179→
   180→    # Remove per-user network if this was the last active sandbox
   181→    begin
   182→      NetworkManager.new.cleanup_user_network(sandbox.user)
   183→    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
   184→      Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager#destroy: network cleanup failed for #{sandbox.user.name}: #{e.message}")
   185→    end
   186→  end
   187→
   188→  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   189→  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   190→  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   191→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   192→
   193→    user = sandbox.user
   194→
   195→    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   196→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   197→
   198→    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   199→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   200→
   201→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   202→    # Strip the timestamp prefix added during archival (e.g. "20260307123456-mybox" → "mybox")
   203→    original_name = sandbox.name.sub(/\A\d{14}-/, "")
   204→    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil, name: original_name)
   205→
   206→    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   207→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   208→    end
   209→
   210→    sandbox
   211→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   212→    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   213→  end
   214→
   215→  def start(sandbox:)
   216→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   217→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   218→
   219→    user = sandbox.user
   220→
   221→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   222→      begin
   223→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   224→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   225→        old.delete(force: true)
   226→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   227→        # already gone
   228→      end
   229→    end
   230→
   231→    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   232→    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
   233→    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
   234→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   235→
   236→    # Refresh image info in case the image was updated since sandbox creation
   237→    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   238→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   239→
   240→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   241→
   242→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   243→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   244→
   245→    sandbox
   246→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   247→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   248→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   249→  end
   250→
   251→  def stop(sandbox:)
   252→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   253→
   254→    begin
   255→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   256→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   257→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   258→    end
   259→
   260→    begin
   261→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   262→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   263→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   264→    end
   265→
   266→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   267→
   268→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→      begin
   270→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   271→        container.stop(t: 10)
   272→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   273→        # already gone
   274→      end
   275→    end
   276→
   277→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   278→    sandbox
   279→  end
   280→
   281→  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze
   282→
   283→  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
   284→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   285→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   286→
   287→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288→    user = sandbox.user.name
   289→
   290→    case service
   291→    when "docker"
   292→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
   293→    when "vnc"
   294→      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
   295→      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
   296→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
   297→    end
   298→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   299→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   300→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   301→    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
   302→  end
   303→
   304→  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
   305→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   306→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   307→
   308→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   309→
   310→    case service
   311→    when "docker"
   312→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   313→    when "vnc"
   314→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   315→    end
   316→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   317→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   318→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   319→    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
   320→  end
   321→
   322→  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   323→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   324→
   325→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   326→    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   327→      .force_encoding("UTF-8").encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace, replace: "")
   328→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   329→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   330→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   331→    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   332→  end
   333→
   334→  def status(sandbox:)
   335→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   336→
   337→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   338→    info = container.json
   339→    {
   340→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   341→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   342→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   343→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   344→    }
   345→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   346→    { state: "not_found" }
   347→  end
   348→
   349→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   350→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   351→  #
   352→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data"
   353→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   354→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   355→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   356→
   357→    user = sandbox.user
   358→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   359→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data]
   360→
   361→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   362→      user: user,
   363→      name: name,
   364→      label: label,
   365→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   366→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   367→    )
   368→
   369→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   370→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   371→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   372→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   373→      image = container.commit(
   374→        repo: repo,
   375→        tag: name,
   376→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   377→      )
   378→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   379→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   380→    end
   381→
   382→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   383→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   384→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   385→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   386→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   387→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   388→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   389→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   390→      end
   391→    end
   392→
   393→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   394→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   395→      if data_subdir.present?
   396→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   397→      else
   398→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   399→      end
   400→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   401→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   402→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   403→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   404→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   405→      end
   406→    end
   407→
   408→    snap.save!
   409→    snap
   410→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   411→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   412→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   413→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   414→  end
   415→
   416→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   417→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   418→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   419→    {
   420→      name: snap.name,
   421→      image: snap.docker_image,
   422→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   423→      created_at: snap.created_at
   424→    }
   425→  end
   426→
   427→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   428→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   429→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   430→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   431→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   432→  end
   433→
   434→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   435→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   436→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   437→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   438→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   439→  end
   440→
   441→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   442→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   443→
   444→    if snap
   445→      # Remove Docker image
   446→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   447→        begin
   448→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   449→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   450→          # Already gone
   451→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   452→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   453→        end
   454→      end
   455→
   456→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   457→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   458→        begin
   459→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   460→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   461→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   462→        end
   463→      end
   464→
   465→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   466→        begin
   467→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   468→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   469→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   470→        end
   471→      end
   472→
   473→      snap.destroy!
   474→    else
   475→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   476→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   477→      begin
   478→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   479→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   480→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   481→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   482→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   483→      end
   484→    end
   485→  end
   486→
   487→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   488→    user = sandbox.user
   489→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   490→
   491→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   492→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   493→
   494→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   495→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   496→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   497→    else
   498→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   499→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   500→    end
   501→
   502→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   503→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   504→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   505→
   506→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   507→    if restore_container
   508→      begin
   509→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   510→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   511→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   512→      end
   513→    end
   514→
   515→    begin
   516→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   517→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   518→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   519→    end
   520→
   521→    begin
   522→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   523→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   524→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   525→    end
   526→
   527→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   528→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   529→    end
   530→
   531→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   532→      begin
   533→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   534→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   535→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   536→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   537→        # Already gone
   538→      end
   539→    end
   540→
   541→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   542→    if restore_home
   543→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   544→      begin
   545→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   546→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   547→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   548→      end
   549→    end
   550→
   551→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   552→    if restore_data
   553→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   554→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   555→      else
   556→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   557→      end
   558→      begin
   559→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   560→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   561→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   562→      end
   563→    end
   564→
   565→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   566→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   567→    # Clear stale container reference before recreating — prevents the record
   568→    # from pointing at a dead container if create_container_and_start fails.
   569→    sandbox.update!(image: final_image, container_id: nil, status: "pending")
   570→
   571→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   572→
   573→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   574→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   575→    end
   576→
   577→    sandbox
   578→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   579→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   580→  end
   581→
   582→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   583→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   584→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   585→
   586→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   587→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   588→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   589→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   590→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   591→        next if tag_name.blank?
   592→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   593→
   594→        Snapshot.create!(
   595→          user: user,
   596→          name: tag_name,
   597→          docker_image: tag,
   598→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   599→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   600→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   601→        )
   602→      end
   603→    end
   604→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   605→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   606→  end
   607→
   608→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   609→    {
   610→      name: snap.name,
   611→      label: snap.label,
   612→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   613→      layers: snap.layers,
   614→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   615→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   616→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   617→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   618→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   619→      created_at: snap.created_at
   620→    }
   621→  end
